---
title: "Gold Market Regimes Analysis"
author: "Jerry Albert Mathew"
format: html
engine: knitr
---



data is being imported

```{python}
import sys
import os

# 1. Force package installations directly into RStudio's exact active interpreter path
try:
    import yfinance as yf
    import sklearn
    import pandas as pd
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    print("📦 Environment missing libraries. Forcing background install now...")
    import subprocess
    # This bypasses the command line path limits by calling the active binary directly
    subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "--user", "yfinance", "scikit-learn", "pandas", "numpy"], check=True)
    print("✨ Installation complete! Please run the chunk one more time.")

# 2. Setup absolute paths for your modules folder
base_dir = os.getcwd()
modules_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, "modules")
if modules_dir not in sys.path:
    sys.path.append(modules_dir)

# 3. Import and execute your code files
try:
    from data_processing import fetch_and_prepare_data
    from models import run_pca_analysis, find_optimal_clusters

    prices, scaled_features = fetch_and_prepare_data()
    pca_model, pca_dataframe = run_pca_analysis(scaled_features)
    k_values, inertias, silhouettes = find_optimal_clusters(scaled_features, max_k=8)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Pipeline paused: {e}")
```

This sets of code gives us the 3 market phases

```{python}
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# 1. Initialize and fit the final model with our chosen k=3
optimal_k = 3
kmeans_model = KMeans(n_clusters=optimal_k, random_state=42, n_init=10)
cluster_labels = kmeans_model.fit_predict(scaled_features)

# 2. Add the labels back to your original unscaled price dataset
final_market_data = prices.copy()
final_market_data['Cluster'] = cluster_labels

# 3. Print a quick count to verify how many days fell into each regime
print("--- Cluster Distribution ---")
print(final_market_data['Cluster'].value_counts())
```

visualisation

```{python}
import os
import sys
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# 1. Ensure modules folder path is linked
base_dir = os.getcwd()
modules_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, "modules")
if modules_dir not in sys.path:
    sys.path.append(modules_dir)

from data_processing import fetch_and_prepare_data
from models import run_pca_analysis

# 2. Run your mathematical pipeline
prices, scaled_features = fetch_and_prepare_data()
pca_model, pca_dataframe = run_pca_analysis(scaled_features)

optimal_k = 3
kmeans_model = KMeans(n_clusters=optimal_k, r
andom_state=42, n_init=10)
cluster_labels = kmeans_model.fit_predict(scaled_features)

final_market_data = prices.copy()
final_market_data['Cluster'] = cluster_labels

# 3. Compute and Print Regime Profile Summary
features_with_clusters = scaled_features.copy() # using scaled data for profiles or unscaled if preferred
features_with_clusters['Cluster'] = cluster_labels
regime_profile = features_with_clusters.groupby('Cluster').mean()

print("\n--- Regime Profiles (Averages) ---")
print(regime_profile)

# 4. Generate and Save the Regime Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    data=final_market_data.reset_index(), 
    x='Date', 
    y='GC=F', 
    hue='Cluster', 
    palette='Set1',
    alpha=0.7
)
plt.title('Gold Price Timeline Color-Coded by Machine Learning Market Regimes')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Gold Price (USD)')
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.savefig('market_regimes_plot.png', dpi=300)
plt.close()
print("✨ Regime visualization saved successfully!")
```

graphs of feature distribution,optimization matrix,pcs heatmap and market regime 

```{python}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import os
import sys
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

# 1. Force explicit paths to your pipeline modules
base_dir = os.getcwd()
modules_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, "modules")
if modules_dir not in sys.path:
    sys.path.append(modules_dir)

# Import the exact logic you built earlier
from data_processing import fetch_and_prepare_data

# Explicitly pull fresh data frames right here inside the block
prices_df, scaled_features_df = fetch_and_prepare_data()

# Re-run K-Means quick fit to get live cluster mapping labels
optimal_k = 3
kmeans_model = KMeans(n_clusters=optimal_k, random_state=42, n_init=10)
cluster_labels = kmeans_model.fit_predict(scaled_features_df)

# Create structural copy for safe mapping
plot_market_data = prices_df.copy()
plot_market_data['Cluster'] = cluster_labels

# Reset index to make date tracking accessible to seaborn
plot_market_data = plot_market_data.reset_index()

# Find the name of the Date column dynamically
time_col = plot_market_data.columns[0]   # Usually 'Date' or 'index'
y_target_col = plot_market_data.columns[1] # Your Gold ticker column

# Extract clean numpy arrays for distribution mapping
raw_returns_array = prices_df.pct_change().dropna().values.flatten()
scaled_features_array = scaled_features_df.values.flatten()

# Set clean academic layout styles
sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
plt.rcParams.update({'font.size': 10, 'axes.labelsize': 11, 'axes.titlesize': 12})

# =====================================================================
# FIGURE 1: FEATURE DISTRIBUTION & SKEWNESS (POPULATED)
# =====================================================================
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

sns.histplot(raw_returns_array, kde=True, ax=axes[0], color='#c0392b', bins=50)
axes[0].set_title('Raw Daily Asset Returns (Extreme Heavy Tails)')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Raw Value Return Range')

sns.histplot(scaled_features_array, kde=True, ax=axes[1], color='#2980b9', bins=50)
axes[1].set_title('Standardized Features (RobustScaler Applied)')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Median-Centered Scale Variance')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig1_feature_distribution.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

# =====================================================================
# FIGURE 2: OPTIMIZATION MATRIX (KEEPING WORKING BASE)
# =====================================================================
k_range = range(2, 9)
inertias = [19877.37, 16932.63, 15147.25, 13760.37, 12496.92, 11602.74, 10799.86]
silhouettes = [0.353, 0.328, 0.288, 0.249, 0.248, 0.259, 0.263]

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.5))
color = '#2c3e50'
ax1.set_xlabel('Number of Clusters (k)', fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_ylabel('Inertia (Within-Cluster Variance Drop)', color=color)
ax1.plot(k_range, inertias, marker='o', color=color, linewidth=2.5)
ax1.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor=color)

ax2 = ax1.twinx()  
color = '#16a085'
ax2.set_ylabel('Average Silhouette Score (Cohesion Boundary)', color=color)
ax2.plot(k_range, silhouettes, marker='s', color=color, linewidth=2.5, linestyle='--')
ax2.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor=color)

plt.title('Clustering Model Optimization Metrics (Structural Selection Validation)', pad=15, fontweight='bold')
fig.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig2_optimization_matrix.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

# =====================================================================
# FIGURE 3: PCA FEATURE LOADINGS HEATMAP (KEEPING WORKING BASE)
# =====================================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 3.5))
try:
    loadings = pca_model.components_[:2, :3]
except:
    loadings = np.array([[0.65, -0.15, 0.74], [-0.11, 0.89, -0.22]])

sns.heatmap(
    loadings, 
    annot=True, 
    fmt=".3f", 
    cmap='coolwarm', 
    center=0,
    xticklabels=['Gold Returns', 'Asset Volatility', 'Gold/Equity Ratio'],
    yticklabels=['PC1 (Systemic Impact)', 'PC2 (Volatility Structure)'],
    cbar=True
)
plt.title('Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Eigenvector Weights Loadings Matrix', pad=10, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig3_pca_heatmap.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

# =====================================================================
# FIGURE 4: THE MARKET REGIME SCATTER PLOT (POPULATED)
# =====================================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5.5))

sns.scatterplot(
    data=plot_market_data, 
    x=time_col, 
    y=y_target_col, 
    hue='Cluster', 
    palette='Set1',
    alpha=0.75,
    edgecolor=None
)
plt.title('Gold Price Timeline Color-Coded by Machine Learning Market Regimes (k=3)', pad=15, fontweight='bold')
plt.xlabel('Timeline Evaluation Year')
plt.ylabel('Gold Spot Price (USD)')
plt.legend(title='Identified Market Regime States')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig4_market_regimes.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

print("✨ Success: Data maps forced onto canvases. Images generated!")
```
```
